one_time_description: Prior period adjustment to restate comparative information due to reclassification of controlled entities from GBE to OGO, reclassification of restricted cash and marketable securities, and changes in portfolio investment and limited partnership accounting methods
economic_notes: First Nation has 33.6% shared control over Glenmor Equipment LP, an agricultural implement dealer, proportionately consolidated. Portfolio investments in Prince Albert First Nations Business Development Limited Partnership (8.33%) and Prince Albert Development Corporation (8.33%). Bison operations, store operations, and Amisk Adventures noted.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 statements were audited and released August 9, 2022 - over two years late. Significant prior period restatement applied to 2019 comparatives (Note 3) due to reclassification of multiple controlled entities. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Cash figure of 2,109,097 is calculated as total cash and restricted cash of 7,169,418 minus restricted cash of 5,060,321. CMHC line shows 0 for 2020 (was 83,983 in 2019 restated). OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC (16,349,029), PAGC (2,419,535), NLCDC (71,070), CMHC subsidy (471,909), SIIT (250,609), Revenue from Other Government Organizations (8,666,404) = 9,283,851; however SIGA and CMHC may also be government transfers - total government transfers estimated including SIGA (901,220) giving OSR of 9,253,711. Glenmor Equipment LP expenses of 7,101,860 included in Other Programs segment expenses. No budget was approved by Chief and Council for the year.
